title: SEC0: wire_notify_callback forget() leaks one closure per set_event_callback call
description: File: wasm/src/client.rs, lines 119-251, specifically line 250: notify_closure.forget(). The wire_notify_callback function is called every time set_event_callback is called. Each call creates a new Closure and calls forget() on it, leaking it permanently into the JS heap. If a JS caller replaces the event callback N times over a session, N closures are leaked. The previous closure is no longer reachable from the transport's notify_cb (it was replaced), so it will never fire again — but the memory is never reclaimed. For a long-lived page this accumulates. The fix is to store the Closure in a field on NieRelayClient so it is dropped when replaced (which requires NieRelayClient to hold the Closure as a field) or to use Closure::into_js_value() and manage the JS reference explicitly.
